us-politics.headline: RFK Jr. Sparks Controversy in Congressional Hearings
us-politics.body:
Independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. faced intense scrutiny during back-to-back congressional hearings on Thursday. A heated exchange erupted between Kennedy and a Democratic lawmaker over controversial comments about Black children - highlighting the candidate's ongoing challenges with messaging and credibility.

The hearings exposed deep divisions, with Kennedy grilled on his long-standing vaccine skepticism and his nascent Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement. His provocative statements continue to draw sharp criticism from both Democratic and Republican lawmakers, underscoring the polarizing nature of his campaign.

sources.title: RFK Jr. grilled over vaccines, MAHA in back-to-back hearings: Key takeaways
Shouting match erupts between RFK Jr and Dem lawmaker over his comments about Black children

us-politics.headline: Trump Reshapes Federal Leadership with Strategic Nominations
us-politics.body:
President Trump moved to reshape federal leadership on Thursday, nominating former Deputy Surgeon General Erica Schwartz as CDC director. The selection appears designed to navigate the complex landscape of vaccine policy - avoiding the most controversial vaccine skeptics while signaling a new approach to public health leadership.

Simultaneously, Trump continues to make strategic appointments across government agencies, with congressional hearings and nominations highlighting his ongoing efforts to restructure federal institutions. These moves reflect a broader pattern of administrative reshaping that has characterized his current political approach.

sources.title: Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief
Trump officially taps Erica Schwartz to lead CDC, avoiding vaccine skeptics
Trump picks former Deputy Surgeon General Erica Schwartz as new CDC nominee

us-politics.headline: US-Israel Diplomacy Secures Temporary Lebanon Ceasefire
us-politics.body:
A 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon began Thursday after diplomatic negotiations backed by the United States. The agreement represents a delicate diplomatic breakthrough in a region marked by persistent tensions.

Reactions were mixed, with some lawmakers expressing caution. Senator Lindsey Graham warned against potentially empowering Hezbollah, suggesting the deal could be strategically complex. Meanwhile, the White House framed the ceasefire as a significant step toward regional stability - highlighting the nuanced diplomatic challenges in the Middle East.

sources.title: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins as Israel agrees to U.S.-backed deal
Graham on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire: ‘Last thing’ US needs is to ‘throw Hezbollah a lifeline’
Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Deepens as Geelong Refinery Fire Sparks Concern
au-politics.body:
A fire at the Geelong refinery has intensified Australia's ongoing fuel supply challenges. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese visited the site, with Viva Energy expressing hope for minimal disruption to national fuel stocks. The incident comes amid rising tensions over fuel availability and potential supply chain vulnerabilities.

The crisis has prompted urgent discussions about Australia's energy resilience. Federal and state officials are working to assess the potential long-term impacts on fuel prices and national energy security.

sources.title: News live: Trump renews accusations Australia hasn’t done enough to help in strait of Hormuz; PM to visit Geelong refinery after fire
Live: PM visits refinery after fire, Viva hopeful of 'minimal' disruption
Fires, deals and flights: Essential updates on Australia’s fuel crisis
Geelong’s refinery fire may be out – but questions over what it means for our fuel crisis are still raging

au-politics.headline: Trump Criticizes Australia's Role in Hormuz Strait Tensions
au-politics.body:
Former US President Donald Trump has renewed accusations that Australia is not doing enough to support operations in the Strait of Hormuz. His comments have reignited diplomatic tensions and raised questions about Australia's international maritime commitments.

The ongoing dispute highlights the complex geopolitical challenges facing Australia's foreign relations. Both government and opposition are carefully navigating the diplomatic landscape, seeking to balance international obligations with national interests.

sources.title: News live: Trump renews accusations Australia hasn’t done enough to help in strait of Hormuz; PM to visit Geelong refinery after fire
Trump repeats he is 'not happy with Australia' as Hormuz crisis continues

au-politics.headline: NSW Protest Laws Face Legal Challenge Over Civil Liberties
au-politics.body:
Legal experts are exploring potential damages claims against controversial New South Wales protest laws. The investigation centers on the laws' impact on civil liberties and potential constitutional challenges.

The move signals growing concern about legislative restrictions on public demonstration. Lawyers are carefully examining the legal grounds for challenging what they view as overly restrictive legislation that may infringe on fundamental rights to peaceful protest.

sources.title: Lawyers explore damages claims over controversial NSW protest laws
Migration debate deserves less politicking from the Liberals

world.headline: Lebanon-Israel Ceasefire Holds - Fragile Peace Takes Shape
world.body:
A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon emerged Thursday, brokered with US involvement. The agreement comes after months of escalating tensions and cross-border strikes that threatened wider regional conflict.

Displaced Lebanese residents remain skeptical about the truce's sustainability. Hezbollah launched final strikes into northern Israel just before the ceasefire announcement - a provocative final gesture signaling continued underlying tensions.

sources.title: Israel and Lebanon agree 10-day ceasefire, Trump says
A ceasefire in Lebanon — will it hold?
Displaced Lebanese in Beirut uncertain if they can trust ceasefire
Hezbollah strikes north Israel ahead of Lebanon ceasefire announcement

world.headline: Netflix Founder Hastings Steps Down After Major Studio Loss
world.body:
Reed Hastings will exit Netflix following the company's failure to renew its critical Warner Bros content deal. The streaming pioneer - who transformed entertainment consumption globally - faces mounting challenges in an increasingly competitive media landscape.

The departure signals potential strategic shifts for Netflix, which has struggled to maintain its dominant market position against rising competitors like Disney+ and Amazon Prime Video.

sources.title: Netflix cofounder Hastings to step down after it lost Warner Bros deal

world.headline: US House Extends Haiti Protections - Trump Policy Challenged
world.body:
Congressional representatives voted to extend temporary protections for Haitian immigrants - a direct rebuke to previous Trump-era immigration restrictions. The move signals potential shifts in US immigration policy and humanitarian approaches.

The vote underscores ongoing political tensions around immigration and represents a significant legislative pushback against more restrictive border policies.

sources.title: US House votes to extend temporary protections for Haitians in Trump rebuke
AOC, Boebert join calls for sexual misconduct accountability in Congress

ai.headline: OpenAI Expands AI Capabilities with Biology and Coding Tools
ai.body:
OpenAI is pushing into specialized AI domains with two significant launches. The company introduced a biology-tuned large language model designed to accelerate life sciences research. Simultaneously, they've unveiled an enhanced Codex system that provides deeper integration with desktop computing - potentially giving AI more control over user systems.

The moves signal OpenAI's strategy to move beyond generalist AI, targeting specific professional domains with precision tools. By developing domain-specific models, the company aims to provide more powerful and targeted solutions for researchers and developers.

sources.title: OpenAI starts offering a biology-tuned LLM
GPT‑Rosalind for life sciences research
OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
New Codex features include the ability to use your computer in the background
Codex for almost everything
OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Claude Code

ai.headline: UK Launches $675M Sovereign AI Fund amid Global Tech Competition
ai.body:
The United Kingdom has committed $675 million to establish a national AI infrastructure fund - a bold move in the escalating global technology race. The investment aims to develop sovereign AI capabilities and reduce dependence on international tech giants.

This strategic funding comes amid growing concerns about technological sovereignty and the potential geopolitical implications of AI development. The fund represents a significant bet on domestic innovation and a desire to position the UK as a competitive player in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

sources.title: The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
The Beginning of Scarcity in AI

ai.headline: AI Startups Attract Massive Valuations and Cutting-Edge Robotics
ai.body:
The AI startup ecosystem continues to surge with remarkable developments. Upscale AI is reportedly in talks for a $2 billion valuation - highlighting investor confidence in the sector. Meanwhile, Physical Intelligence has demonstrated a breakthrough robotics system capable of learning tasks without explicit prior training.

These advances underscore the rapid pace of AI innovation. Startups are pushing boundaries in machine learning, showing increasingly sophisticated capabilities that challenge traditional assumptions about artificial intelligence and machine adaptability.

sources.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught

btc.headline: Charles Schwab Launches Retail Bitcoin Trading Platform
btc.body: Charles Schwab is set to roll out spot Bitcoin trading for retail clients, marking a significant milestone in mainstream cryptocurrency adoption. The financial giant's move signals growing institutional acceptance of Bitcoin as an investable asset. Retail investors will soon have direct access to Bitcoin trading through a major traditional brokerage platform - a development that could dramatically expand Bitcoin's investor base.

sources.title: Charles Schwab Weighs Prediction Markets Move as Bitcoin, Ethereum Trading Nears
Charles Schwab to roll out spot Bitcoin, Ether trading for retail clients
Charles Schwab to Launch Spot Bitcoin Trading for Retail Clients
Charles Schwab begins rollout of spot bitcoin, ethereum trading platform

btc.headline: Bitcoin Quantum Debate Heats Up Among Tech Experts
btc.body: Bitcoin developers and crypto leaders are locked in a heated discussion about potential quantum computing threats to the network. Adam Back is pushing for optional upgrades to make Bitcoin quantum-proof, while Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson argues that proposed solutions won't save existing coins held by Satoshi Nakamoto. The technical debate highlights growing concerns about long-term Bitcoin network security in an era of rapidly advancing computational technologies.

sources.title: Quantum Proposal Won’t Save Satoshi’s Bitcoin, Says Cardano Founder Hoskinson
Cardano's Charles Hoskinson says Bitcoin's quantum fix is a hard fork that can't save Satoshi's coins
Adam Back says Bitcoin’s post-quantum shift may reveal true Satoshi stash
Morning Minute: Bitcoin Devs Propose New Quantum Solution
Adam Back Pushes for Optional Upgrades to Quantum-Proof Bitcoin

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $75K as Institutional Interest Grows
btc.body: Bitcoin's price remains volatile - trading around $74,500 with analysts seeing potential for further upside. Institutional interest continues to build, with Bitcoin ETFs adding $186 million in recent days and Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin fund attracting over $100 million in its first week. Public crypto miners are also actively managing their Bitcoin holdings, selling significant volumes in Q1 2026.

sources.title: Bitcoin analysts see ‘further upside,’ and identify next price levels to watch
Bitcoin bull run ‘still too early’ to call as demand lags exiting capital: Analyst
Bitcoin rebounds near $74.5K as US stocks chase after new all-time highs
Bitcoin slides back below $74,000 as breakout to higher levels fails again
Bitcoin ETFs add $186M as Morgan Stanley’s MSBT inflows top $100M in first six trading days
